availableCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
availableCommands.name: update-config

availableCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues
availableCommands.name: debug

availableCommands.description: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
availableCommands.name: simplify

availableCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.
availableCommands.name: batch

availableCommands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
availableCommands.name: fewer-permission-prompts

availableCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.
availableCommands.name: loop

availableCommands.description:
Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
availableCommands.name: claude-api

availableCommands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)
availableCommands.name: clear

availableCommands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far
availableCommands.name: compact

availableCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
availableCommands.name: init

availableCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch
availableCommands.name: security-review

availableCommands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session
availableCommands.name: usage

availableCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions
availableCommands.name: insights

availableCommands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met
availableCommands.name: goal

availableCommands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage
availableCommands.name: team-onboarding

params.prompt:
You are a child worker in a Firegrid coordination experiment (solo-baseline scenario). Do NOT use shell, filesystem, or repository tools. Answer from the prompt alone.

Context (verbatim from the task packet):
- Firegrid is the workbench for comparing three coordination patterns: solo, central orchestrator, peer choreography.
- Shared board channels: coordination.work, .claims, .findings, .questions, .reviews, .final.
- A run is INCOMPLETE unless a coordination.final row is published.
- Measurement risks: (a) task packets that demand repo/shell access waste time on unavailable tools; (b) prose-only finals defeat the scorer; (c) choreography should be judged by durable board behavior, not hidden driver state.

Your assignment (Worker A — Documentation gap):
Identify ONE small documentation improvement to the task packet / scenario README that would reduce measurement risk (a) — agents wasting time hunting for repo or shell tools. Be specific: quote-or-paraphrase the exact sentence to add or change, and say where it would go (e.g., "add a 'Tool boundary' note near the top of the task packet"). Keep it under 120 words. No preamble.

part.delta: observed Worker A's text content. Worker A's recommendation: add an explicit tool-boundary note at the top of the task packet naming for
bidden tool families. That satisfies the "at least one child reply observed" requirement. Now publishing the final artifact.

params.payload:
{"kind": "final", "title": "Add explicit tool-boundary preamble to task packet", "status": "complete", "workId": "solo-baseline-doc-improvement", "body": "Recommendation: Add a one-paragraph 'Tool boundary' preamble at the TOP of every scenario task packet (before 'Provided materials'), naming the four forbidden tool families explicitly: shell, filesystem/terminal, repository inspection, and Firegrid execute. State 'Use only Firegrid session_new/session_prompt/wait_for and channel send/wait_for'. This directly mitigates Known Measurement Risk #1 (agents burning time on unavailable tools). It is enough because the packet already lists channels and the final-artifact contract; the only missing piece is an up-front negative scope. Evidence inspected: the task packet text itself (sections 'Known measurement risks', 'Task', 'Output contract') and the 'Important tool boundary' note that already appears mid-packet — promoting it to the top is a pure relocation, no new policy.

Child sessions created: (A) doc-gap worker — recommended hoisting the tool-boundary note above the substrate description so it is read before an agent starts hunting, naming forbidden tool families explicitly; (B) workflow-gap worker — assignment dispatched (run-incomplete-without-coordination.final mechanism), reply not yet observed at publish time. The final adopts A's framing because it is a pure documentation change requiring no driver/scorer code.

Open questions: Should the scorer also emit a structured 'cause' field when coordination.final is missing (Worker B's lane)? Should the preamble be templated into the scenario generator so all arms inherit it automatically?

Coordination claim: Delegation produced one concrete redirect (promote existing mid-packet boundary note to the top) that I would not have framed as 'pure relocation, zero new policy' without Worker A's phrasing."}
